rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by fixing unresolved references or removing invalid clauses, not by adding architecture
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantee expressions
Align helper declaration types with expression types: bool for comparisons, int for integer arithmetic, real for real arithmetic
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool in const and eq declarations
generic_rules: Minimize repairs to only address validator diagnostics
Preserve existing architecture when repairing AGREE contracts
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Align type declarations with expression types
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture to fix validator diagnostics
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
AADL package references require matching with clauses
Generated AGREE code should not introduce package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references that are absent from the analyzed model and its configured dependencies
